author: śrīla viśvanātha cakrabartī ṭhākura
verses:
saṁsāra⋅dāvānala⋅līḍha⋅loka⋅
trāṇāya kāruṇya⋅ghanāghanatvam
prāptasya kalyāṇa⋅guṇārṇavasya
vande guroḥ śrī⋅caraṇāravindam

mahā⋅prabhoḥ kīrtana⋅nṛtya⋅gīta⋅
vāditra⋅mādyan⋅manaso rasena
romāñca⋅kampāśru⋅taraṅga⋅bhājo
vande guroḥ śrī⋅caraṇāravindam

śrī⋅vigrahārādhana⋅nitya⋅nānā⋅
śṛṅgāra⋅tanmandira⋅mārjanādau
juktasya⋅bhaktāṁś⋅ca⋅niyuñjato’pi
vande guroḥ śrī⋅caraṇāravindam

catur⋅vidha⋅śrī⋅bhagavat⋅prasāda⋅
svādv⋅anna⋅tṛptān hari⋅bhakta⋅saṅghān
kṛtvaiva tṛptiṁ bhajataḥ sadaiva
vande guroḥ śrī⋅caraṇāravindam

śrī⋅rādhikā⋅mādhavayor⋅apāra⋅
mādhurya⋅līlā⋅guṇa⋅rūpa⋅nāmnām
prati⋅kṣaṇāsvādana⋅lolupasya
vande guroḥ śrī⋅caraṇāravindam

nikuñja⋅yūnoḥ⋅rati⋅keli⋅siddhyai
jā jālibhir⋅yuktir⋅apekṣaṇīyā
tatrāti⋅dākṣād⋅ati⋅vallabhasya
vande guroḥ śrī⋅caraṇāravindam

sākṣād⋅dharitvena samasta śāstrair⋅
uktas tathā bhāvyata eva sadbhiḥ
kintu prabhor⋅yaḥ priya eva tasya
vande guroḥ śrī⋅caraṇāravindam

jasya prasādād bhagavat⋅prasādo
jasyā⋅prasādān⋅na gatiḥ kuto’pi
dhyāyan stuvaṁsa tasya jaśas⋅tri⋅sandhyaṁ
vande guroḥ śrī⋅caraṇāravindam

śrīmad⋅guroraṣṭakam⋅etad⋅uccair
brāhme muhūrte paṭhati prayatnāt
jastena vṛndāvana⋅nātha⋅sākṣāt
sevaiva labhyā januṣo’nta eva
en_translation:
I offer obeisances to the lotus feet of the spiritual master, who is like a dense cloud of mercy appearing to save those who are scorched by the blazing forest fire of material existence. He is an ocean of auspicious qualities.

I offer obeisances to the lotus feet of the spiritual master, whose mind is maddened by the nectar of the congregational chanting, dancing, singing, and musical instruments of Lord Caitanya. He is always experiencing waves of ecstatic thrills, trembling, and tears.

I offer obeisances to the lotus feet of the spiritual master, who is engaged in the daily worship of the Deity of the Lord, adorning Them with various ornaments and cleaning Their temple. He also engages the devotees in these services.

I offer obeisances to the lotus feet of the spiritual master, who is always satisfied by seeing the assemblies of devotees contentedly partaking of the four kinds of delicious food offered to the Supreme Lord.

I offer obeisances to the lotus feet of the spiritual master, who is ever greedy to relish, moment after moment, the unlimited sweetness of the pastimes, qualities, forms, and names of Śrī Rādhā and Mādhava.

I offer obeisances to the lotus feet of the spiritual master, who is very expert and dear to the Divine Couple in bringing about the success of Their loving pastimes through whatever arrangements are required by the companions.

I offer obeisances to the lotus feet of the spiritual master, who is directly the Lord Himself according to all the scriptures and is also considered so by the saintly. However, he is the dearmost beloved of the Lord.

I offer obeisances to the lotus feet of the spiritual master. By his mercy, one achieves the mercy of the Lord. Without his mercy, there is no progress toward the destination. Therefore, one should meditate upon and glorify his fame three times a day.

Whoever carefully recites these eight stanzas glorifying the spiritual master loudly during the auspicious hour before dawn will surely attain the direct service of the Lord of Vṛndāvana at the end of this very life.
